file.content:
# CDK — Claude Development Kit

A distributable framework that lets Electron apps drive a user's already-installed
`claude` CLI. Zero auth handling — the CLI resolves its own credentials.

> **Status:** Phase 0 scaffold. See [DESIGN.md](./DESIGN.md) for the full plan.

## Packages

- **`@claude-cdk/core`** — process spawn, stream parser, session manager, event types. No Electron dependency.
- **`@claude-cdk/electron-host`** — `ipcMain` glue around core.
- **`@claude-cdk/electron-client`** — renderer-process client over IPC.

## Development

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm -r build
pnpm -r test
```

Requires Node 20+ and `pnpm` (enable via `corepack enable`).

1	# CDK — Claude Development Kit
2	
3	A distributable framework that lets Electron apps drive a user's already-installed
4	Claude Code CLI without touching auth, OAuth, or API keys. Each consumer
5	authenticates `claude` themselves; CDK is purely a process driver and event bus.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Goals
10	
11	- Distributable npm package consumed by multiple Electron apps
12	- Zero auth handling — the CLI resolves its own credentials
13	- Stream-first event model (no fake request/response over a streaming protocol)
14	- Clean main-process / renderer-process split for Electron
15	- Forward-compatible with CLI updates
16	
17	## Non-goals (initial release)
18	
19	- API key management
20	- Hosted / server-side usage
21	- Replacing the official Agent SDK for non-Electron contexts
22	- Modeling or persisting transcripts (the CLI already does this in `~/.claude/projects/`)
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Architecture
27	
28	Monorepo with three packages:
29	
30	- **`@claude-cdk/core`** — process spawn, stream parser, session manager, event types. No Electron dependency. Independently usable from any Node script.
31	- **`@claude-cdk/electron-host`** — thin glue that registers `ipcMain` handlers around core.
32	- **`@claude-cdk/electron-client`** — renderer-process client speaking to the host over IPC, exposing the same shape as core.
33	
34	Keeping core Electron-free is the single most important architectural choice.
35	It makes the package testable without spinning up Electron and lets non-Electron
36	consumers benefit later if you want.
37	
38	---
39	
40	## Event Taxonomy
41	
42	All events share a base shape:
43	
44	```ts
45	interface BaseEvent {
46	  type: string;        // discriminator
47	  sessionId: string;   // which session
48	  turnId: string;      // which turn within the session
49	  seq: number;         // monotonic per-session sequence number
50	  ts: number;          // ms epoch

file.content:
# CDK — Claude Development Kit

A distributable framework that lets Electron apps drive a user's already-installed
Claude Code CLI without touching auth, OAuth, or API keys. Each consumer
authenticates `claude` themselves; CDK is purely a process driver and event bus.

---

## Goals

- Distributable npm package consumed by multiple Electron apps
- Zero auth handling — the CLI resolves its own credentials
- Stream-first event model (no fake request/response over a streaming protocol)
- Clean main-process / renderer-process split for Electron
- Forward-compatible with CLI updates

## Non-goals (initial release)

- API key management
- Hosted / server-side usage
- Replacing the official Agent SDK for non-Electron contexts
- Modeling or persisting transcripts (the CLI already does this in `~/.claude/projects/`)

---

## Architecture

Monorepo with three packages:

- **`@claude-cdk/core`** — process spawn, stream parser, session manager, event types. No Electron dependency. Independently usable from any Node script.
- **`@claude-cdk/electron-host`** — thin glue that registers `ipcMain` handlers around core.
- **`@claude-cdk/electron-client`** — renderer-process client speaking to the host over IPC, exposing the same shape as core.

Keeping core Electron-free is the single most important architectural choice.
It makes the package testable without spinning up Electron and lets non-Electron
consumers benefit later if you want.

---

## Event Taxonomy

All events share a base shape:

```ts
interface BaseEvent {
  type: string;        // discriminator
  sessionId: string;   // which session
  turnId: string;      // which turn within the session
  seq: number;         // monotonic per-session sequence number
  ts: number;          // ms epoch
